design_philosophy.core_loop: Combat-focused progression through multiple play styles: side-scrolling beat 'em up levels, ranged 'run and gun' Batarang sections, and vehicle-based shooting stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Multi-threat combat encounters requiring adaptation between melee, ranged, and vehicular gameplay modes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying Batman across varied combat scenarios from the 1992 film, utilizing different tools (hand-to-hand, Batarangs, Batmobile/Batskiboat) to overcome enemies
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Konami enforced high difficulty with explicit messaging encouraging players to attempt harder difficulty settings; game includes 'Mania' difficulty tier with special completion recognition. Features hidden stage select, indicating developer expectation of player skill-building or alternative progression paths

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple difficulty tiers available; western versions feature post-credit screens urging players to retry on higher difficulty settings with special 'True Dark Knight' message for Mania completion. Hidden stage select code allows player choice of progression order

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-based progression with hidden stage select accessible via Game Genie/Pro Action Replay codes, allowing skilled players to bypass intended linear sequence
replayability_hooks: Multiple difficulty tiers with escalating challenge
Mania difficulty tier with unique completion recognition
Hidden stage select enables non-linear progression experimentation
Regional differences in cutscene sequencing (Japanese vs. western versions)

world_building_techniques: Film-based narrative adaptation following 1992 Batman Returns movie plot
Cutscene transitions between stages with character interactions (e.g., Catwoman defeat sequences with regional variations)
Post-credit narrative messaging encouraging continued play on harder difficulties

boss_design.philosophy: Notable single-character encounters integrated into narrative progression, particularly Catwoman as recurring boss threat
notable_bosses: Catwoman - Features two distinct encounters with regional narrative differences; first battle shows contrasting scene transition sequences between Japanese and western versions regarding her defeat and building departure

faction_indicators: Film-licensed property anchoring player identity to Batman faction
Multiple playstyle options (melee, ranged, vehicular) enabling varied self-expression within criminal-fighting fantasy
danger_signals: High difficulty explicitly positioned as core challenge with Mania tier as ultimate achievement marker
Post-credit messaging suggesting incomplete experience if not attempting higher difficulties
Single-life runs on Atari Lynx version establish permadeath expectations in some ports
environmental_storytelling: Varied level environments reflecting film locations and set pieces
Batmobile and Batskiboat vehicle sections embedding transportation-based level design
Narrative stage transitions through cutscenes depicting Batman's progression through Gotham

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Batman Returns (SNES) entry documenting stage select, regional differences, and Catwoman encounter variations
Wikipedia - List of video games featuring Batman - SNES entry describing gameplay features, multiple play styles, and difficulty positioning
